title: Peruvian Roast Chicken

overview.description: Peruvian-style roast chicken marinated with garlic, spices, vinegar and citrus, then roasted until deeply flavoured and juicy.

method: Mix the garlic, soy sauce, vinegar, lime juice, olive oil, cumin, paprika, oregano, coriander, black pepper, salt, cinnamon and cloves.
Rub the marinade thoroughly over the chicken, including under the skin where possible.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, preferably overnight.
Remove the chicken from the refrigerator approximately 30 minutes before cooking.
Place the chicken breast-side up in a roasting tray.
Roast for approximately 1 hour 15 minutes to 1 hour 30 minutes, depending on the size of the chicken.
Baste once or twice during cooking with the pan juices.
Check that the thickest part of the thigh reaches 74°C.
Rest the chicken for 15–20 minutes before carving.

learning.did-you-know: Pollo a la brasa is one of Peru's most popular dishes and was originally developed in Lima. The traditional restaurant version is roasted over charcoal and is usually served with fries and a distinctive green sauce.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to marinate a whole chicken, build layers of flavour with spices and acid, and roast a bird evenly while keeping the meat juicy.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A distinctive South American roast chicken that introduces the children to a completely different spice profile while making excellent use of a whole chicken.
chapman-tips: Marinate overnight if possible; the flavour improves significantly.
Getting some marinade under the breast skin gives the meat much more flavour.
Pat the skin dry before roasting if you want it to become especially crisp.
Rest the chicken properly before carving.
Save the carcass for chicken stock after the meal.
notes.batch-cooking: Cooked chicken freezes well. Strip the meat from the bones, allow it to cool completely and freeze in portions with a little of the cooking juices.
notes.leftovers: Use leftover chicken in sandwiches, wraps, rice bowls, salads, tacos or soups. Save the carcass for chicken stock.
